TB Diagnosis + Drug Susceptibility (CDC + WHO):

(1) Initial diagnostics: AFB smear (sensitivity 50-70%, fast), TB culture (gold standard but slow 4-8 wk), NAAT (Xpert MTB/RIF — rapid + detects rifampin resistance)
detailed_explanation.reason: TB pathology: AFB + culture + NAAT (Xpert) + DST essential. DS standard 6-month regimen or 4-month new. MDR — BPaL revolutionary 6-month all-oral. DOT + isolation + public health reporting. Contact tracing + LTBI treatment. Multidisciplinary.

detailed_explanation.reason: CLL: CD5+ CD19+ CD23+ classic. Risk by IGHV + cytogenetics. Modern treatment: BTK inhibitors + venetoclax — targeted, fixed duration + improved outcomes. detailed_explanation.reason: Transfusion reactions: AHTR most serious (ABO incompatibility, often clerical error). detailed_explanation.summary:
Ductal Carcinoma In Situ (DCIS):

(1) Definition: malignant epithelial proliferation confined ใน ductal-lobular system โดย basement membrane intact
detailed_explanation.reason: DCIS — pre-invasive ductal neoplasia, myoepithelium intact (p63+/calponin+). BCS+RT or mastectomy; SLN เฉพาะ selected. Endocrine therapy ลด recurrence ใน ER+. Margin ≥ 2 mm.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยหญิงอายุ 62 ปี — palpable mass at breast → core biopsy: cells in single-file (Indian file) pattern infiltrating stroma + signet ring cells + lack of cohesion; IHC: ER+ PR+ HER2 0, **E-cadherin negative**, p120 cytoplasmic relocation; การวินิจฉัย
reference_source: WHO Breast Tumors 5th ed; CAP Breast Cancer Protocol

detailed_explanation.summary:
Invasive Lobular Carcinoma (ILC):

(1) Distinctive histology: discohesive cells, single-file (Indian file) pattern, targetoid arrangement around ducts, signet ring cells, low-grade nuclei most cases
detailed_explanation.reason: ILC: discohesive single-file, E-cadherin loss (CDH1), p120 cytoplasmic. Almost always ER+/HER2-. Imaging occult; MRI helpful. Unique metastasis pattern (GI, ovary, LM). Endocrine mainstay. CDH1 germline → HDGC syndrome.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยชายอายุ 65 ปี — endoscopic biopsy of gastric ulcer: glands with intestinal differentiation + goblet cells + cohesive nests, vs another patient with poorly cohesive cells + signet ring + diffuse infiltration; การจำแนกตาม Lauren classification และ implications
reference_source: WHO Digestive System Tumors 5th ed; NCCN Gastric Cancer; ToGA

detailed_explanation.summary:
Gastric Adenocarcinoma — Lauren Classification:

(1) Intestinal type: cohesive glands, intestinal metaplasia background, expansive growth, distal stomach, older men, environmental + H
detailed_explanation.reason: Gastric ca Lauren: intestinal (cohesive, H. pylori-driven, Correa cascade) vs diffuse (signet ring, CDH1 loss, worse). TCGA: EBV+/MSI/GS/CIN. Biomarkers: HER2, PD-L1 CPS, MSI, EBV, claudin 18.2. FLOT + D2 resectable.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 58 ปี — right colon adenocarcinoma resection — IHC: **MLH1 loss + PMS2 loss**, MSH2/MSH6 intact; molecular: BRAF V600E mutation present, MLH1 promoter hypermethylation positive; การ interpret และ implications
reference_source: NCCN CRC; CAP MMR/MSI; WHO Digestive Tumors 5th ed

detailed_explanation.summary:
Colorectal MSI/MMR Interpretation:

(1) MMR IHC pattern: paired loss MLH1+PMS2 OR MSH2+MSH6 (heterodimer partners)
detailed_explanation.reason: MLH1 loss + BRAF V600E + MLH1 methylation = sporadic MSI-H, NOT Lynch. MSH2/MSH6/PMS2 loss → germline directly. MSI-H predicts IO response (frontline metastatic, neoadjuvant rectal). Universal CRC + EC testing.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Hepatocellular Carcinoma (HCC):

(1) Histology: trabecular > 3 cells thick, pseudoglandular, solid, scirrhous patterns
detailed_explanation.reason: HCC: trabecular + bile production, IHC HepPar-1/arginase-1/GPC3/AFP. Subtypes (fibrolamellar DNAJB1-PRKACA). BCLC-based treatment; atezo-bev frontline systemic. HBV/HCV/MASLD risk; surveillance with US±AFP q6mo + LI-RADS.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 65 ปี — pancreatic head mass + obstructive jaundice → EUS-FNA biopsy: malignant glands with mucin in desmoplastic stroma; IHC: CK7+ CK19+ CK20- CDX2-; molecular: KRAS mutation, TP53, CDKN2A, SMAD4 loss; การวินิจฉัย และ implications
reference_source: WHO Digestive Tumors 5th ed; NCCN Pancreatic; NAPOLI-3

detailed_explanation.summary:
Pancreatic Ductal Adenocarcinoma (PDAC):

(1) Histology: malignant glandular structures, mucin production, desmoplastic stroma (fibrous reaction prominent), perineural + lymphovascular invasion common, infiltrative pattern
detailed_explanation.reason: PDAC: glands + desmoplasia + perineural; CK7+/CK19+, SMAD4 loss ~55%. KRAS > 90% + TP53/CDKN2A/SMAD4. Actionable: BRCA/PALB2 (PARP), MSI rare, KRAS G12C. NALIRIFOX + FOLFIRINOX. Germline all patients.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Endometrial Carcinoma Pathology + Molecular Classification:

(1) Histology: endometrioid adenocarcinoma — confluent glands, cribriform, squamous differentiation common, low-grade nuclei (FIGO 1-2) vs serous + clear cell + carcinosarcoma (high grade)
detailed_explanation.reason: Endometrial endometrioid ca: confluent glands + atypia. FIGO 2023 integrates TCGA molecular (POLE/MMRd/p53abn/NSMP) → prognosis + Tx. Pembro + lenvatinib advanced; pembro alone dMMR. Universal Lynch screen; HER2 in serous.

reference_source: WHO Female Genital Tumors 5th ed; NCCN Ovarian; SOLO-1; PAOLA-1

detailed_explanation.summary:
High-Grade Serous Carcinoma (HGSC) — Ovarian/Tubal:

(1) Histology: papillary, slit-like glandular, solid
detailed_explanation.reason: HGSC: papillary + psammoma + p53 aberrant + WT1+/PAX8+. TP53 ~96%; HRD ~50% (BRCA/HR genes). Origin: tubal fimbria (STIC). Treatment: cytoreduction + plat-tax + PARP maintenance (HRD biggest benefit). Universal germline.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยชายอายุ 68 ปี — elevated PSA → prostate biopsy: small infiltrative glands lacking basal cells, nucleoli prominent; ISUP/Gleason score 4+3=7 (Grade group 3); IHC: AMACR (P504S)+, basal markers (p63, HMWCK) negative; PTEN loss; การ stage และ rxtreatment
reference_source: WHO Urinary + Male Genital 5th ed; ISUP 2019; NCCN Prostate; VISION

detailed_explanation.summary:
Prostate Adenocarcinoma — Grading + Management:

(1) Histology: infiltrative small glands lacking basal cells
detailed_explanation.reason: Prostate ca: small glands, no basal, AMACR+/p63-. ISUP grade groups GG1-5. Cribriform/IDC-P adverse. PSMA-PET staging; HRR germline (PARP). Risk-based: AS → RP/RT → ADT+ARSI±chemo → mCRPC ARSI/177Lu-PSMA/PARP.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 60 ปี — renal mass nephrectomy: tumor with clear cytoplasm + delicate vasculature 'chicken wire' pattern + Fuhrman/WHO-ISUP grade 3; IHC: CAIX+, PAX8+, CD10+, vimentin+, CK7 patchy; molecular: VHL inactivation, 3p loss; การวินิจฉัย
reference_source: WHO Urinary + Male Genital 5th ed; NCCN Kidney

detailed_explanation.summary:
Clear Cell Renal Cell Carcinoma (ccRCC):

(1) Histology: clear cytoplasm (glycogen + lipid dissolved during processing), delicate vascular network 'chicken wire', solid/alveolar/cystic
detailed_explanation.reason: ccRCC: clear cytoplasm + chicken-wire vessels; CAIX+/PAX8+/CD10+; VHL inactivation + 3p loss. WHO/ISUP grading. Hereditary: VHL syndrome. Treatment: IO doublet or IO+TKI metastatic; belzutifan HIF-2α inhibitor.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยชายอายุ 70 ปี — gross hematuria + bladder cystoscopy: papillary tumor → TURBT shows papillary urothelial neoplasm with mild atypia + low mitoses + > 7 cells thick; การจำแนกตาม WHO 2022 + ติดตาม
reference_source: WHO Urinary + Male Genital 5th ed; NCCN Bladder; EV-302

detailed_explanation.summary:
Urothelial Carcinoma — Classification + Management:

(1) WHO 2022 (5th ed) classification:
  • Urothelial papilloma (rare)
detailed_explanation.reason: Urothelial ca WHO 2022: PUNLMP/low-grade/high-grade/CIS/invasive. Variants matter (micropap/plasmacytoid aggressive). FGFR3 — erdafitinib. NMIBC: TURBT+BCG. MIBC: neoadj cis + cystectomy. Metastatic: EV+pembro frontline.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยชายอายุ 28 ปี — testicular mass orchiectomy: tumor cells with fried-egg cytoplasm + clear glycogen + lymphocytic infiltrate; IHC: OCT3/4+, PLAP+, CD117+, SALL4+, CK negative; serum: AFP normal, beta-hCG mildly elevated, LDH elevated
reference_source: WHO Urinary + Male Genital 5th ed; NCCN Testicular

detailed_explanation.summary:
Testicular Seminoma (Classic):

(1) Histology: sheets/nests of uniform cells with clear cytoplasm (glycogen — 'fried egg'), prominent central nucleoli, fibrous septa with lymphocytic infiltrate ± granulomas
detailed_explanation.reason: Seminoma: fried-egg + lymphocytic infiltrate; OCT3/4+/CD117+/SALL4+, CK-/CD30-. AFP normal (if AFP↑ → mixed/yolk sac). Precursor GCNIS. Stage I surveillance preferred. BEP chemo highly curative. i(12p) characteristic.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 35 ปี — thyroid nodule FNA → cytology Bethesda VI (malignant); thyroidectomy: papillary architecture + nuclear features (clearing, grooves, intranuclear pseudoinclusions) + psammoma bodies; molecular: BRAF V600E mutation
reference_source: WHO Endocrine Tumors 5th ed; Bethesda 2023; ATA Thyroid 2015

detailed_explanation.summary:
Papillary Thyroid Carcinoma (PTC):

(1) Bethesda system for thyroid cytopathology I-VI
detailed_explanation.reason: PTC: papillary + diagnostic nuclear features (clearing, grooves, pseudoinclusions) + psammoma. Bethesda → molecular refines risk. NIFTP carved out. BRAF V600E ~60%; targeted Tx for advanced (BRAF/RET/NTRK). RAI selected.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยหญิงอายุ 62 ปี non-smoker — lung mass → biopsy: glandular architecture with lepidic + acinar patterns; IHC: TTF-1+, napsin-A+, CK7+; molecular reflex: EGFR exon 19 deletion mutation positive, ALK negative, ROS1 negative, PD-L1 TPS 5%
reference_source: WHO Thoracic Tumors 5th ed; NCCN NSCLC; CAP/IASLC/AMP Lung Biomarker

detailed_explanation.summary:
Lung Adenocarcinoma — Pathology + Molecular:

(1) Histology: lepidic, acinar, papillary, micropapillary (aggressive), solid, mucinous, colloid
detailed_explanation.reason: Lung adenoca: TTF-1+/napsin-A+. IASLC grading. Essential biomarkers (EGFR/ALK/ROS1/BRAF/KRAS/MET/HER2/RET/NTRK/NRG1/PD-L1). Matched targeted Tx (osimertinib EGFR; alectinib ALK; etc.). Re-biopsy at progression.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 70 ปี heavy smoker — central lung mass → biopsy: keratin pearls + intercellular bridges + cells with abundant eosinophilic cytoplasm; IHC: p40+, p63+, CK5/6+, TTF-1 negative; การวินิจฉัย และ implications
reference_source: WHO Thoracic Tumors 5th ed; NCCN NSCLC; KEYNOTE-407

detailed_explanation.summary:
Squamous Cell Carcinoma of Lung:

(1) Histology: keratinization (keratin pearls), intercellular bridges, eosinophilic dense cytoplasm
detailed_explanation.reason: Squamous NSCLC: keratinization + bridges; p40+/p63+/CK5/6+, TTF-1-/napsin-. Smoking-related, few drivers. PD-L1 + NGS. Pembro ± chemo (KEYNOTE-407). No pemetrexed/bev (squamous). Perioperative IO + chemo (CheckMate 816).

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 55 ปี — pigmented skin lesion excision: atypical melanocytes with pagetoid spread + asymmetry + Breslow thickness 2.3 mm + ulceration present + mitoses 4/mm²; IHC: S100+, SOX10+, HMB-45+, Melan-A+; molecular: BRAF V600E mutation positive
reference_source: WHO Skin Tumors 5th ed; AJCC 8th; NCCN Melanoma; RELATIVITY-047

detailed_explanation.summary:
Cutaneous Melanoma — Pathology + Management:

(1) Histology: atypical melanocytes in nests + single cells
detailed_explanation.reason: Melanoma: pagetoid + atypia + Breslow + ulceration + mitoses. SOX10/S100/HMB-45/Melan-A/PRAME. BRAF V600 ~50%; subtypes differ molecularly. SLNB T1b+. Adjuvant IO IIB+; metastatic nivo+ipi or nivo+rela; neoadjuvant pembro emerging.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 72 ปี — pearly papule with telangiectasias on nose × 1 year → shave biopsy: nests of basaloid cells with peripheral palisading + cleft (retraction) artifact + myxoid stroma; IHC: BerEP4+, EMA-, CK20-; การวินิจฉัยและการรักษา
reference_source: WHO Skin Tumors 5th ed; NCCN BCC

detailed_explanation.summary:
Basal Cell Carcinoma (BCC):

(1) Histology: basaloid cells with peripheral palisading + retraction artifact (cleft between epithelium + stroma due to mucin) + mucinous stroma + variable mitoses
detailed_explanation.reason: BCC: basaloid + peripheral palisading + cleft; BerEP4+/BCL2+. HH pathway. Subtypes (nodular/infiltrative/morpheaform). Mohs for high-risk facial. HHi (vismodegib) + cemiplimab for advanced. Gorlin if multiple/young.

scenario: นักศึกษาแพทย์สงสัยหลักการ tissue fixation และ processing: เนื้อเยื่อใส่ใน 10% neutral buffered formalin เกินไป 48 ชม จะมีผลอย่างไร และ alternatives สำหรับ molecular testing
reference_source: CAP Cancer Protocols; ASCO/CAP HER2 + ER Guidelines; AJCP Fixation

detailed_explanation.summary:
Tissue Fixation Principles:

(1) 10% NBF (neutral buffered formalin) = standard — cross-links proteins (methylene bridges) — penetrates ~ 1 mm/h — recommended 6-72 hours fixation (per CAP/ASCO HER2/ER guidelines for breast)
detailed_explanation.reason: 10% NBF standard, 6-72h fixation; cold ischemia ≤1h for breast biomarkers. Over-fix degrades IHC + nucleic acids; under-fix → autolysis. Alternatives: Bouin's (testes/bone), PAXgene (molecular), alcohol (cytology), frozen (IF/molecular). EDTA for bone.

scenario: Resident pathology — ขอเข้าใจหลักการของ immunohistochemistry (IHC) — antigen retrieval + detection systems + controls + interpretation pitfalls

detailed_explanation.summary:
IHC Principles + Pitfalls:

(1) Concept: antibody-based detection of specific antigens in tissue — primary Ab → secondary Ab/polymer with enzyme (HRP/AP) → chromogen (DAB brown, AEC red) → visualized in tissue context
detailed_explanation.reason: IHC: Ab-Ag detection in tissue. HIER (citrate pH6 / EDTA pH9) essential FFPE. Polymer detection sensitive. Controls (positive/negative/internal) every run. Pitfalls: fixation, edge, endogenous biotin/perox, heterogeneity. Quantification standardized (Allred/TPS).

scenario: หลักการของ FISH (fluorescence in situ hybridization) ใน pathology — applications + interpretation + advantages vs limitations เมื่อเทียบกับ IHC และ NGS

detailed_explanation.summary:
FISH Principles + Applications:

(1) Concept: fluorescently labeled DNA probes hybridize to specific chromosomal regions in interphase cells (FFPE tissue) or metaphase (cytogenetics) — detects gene amplifications, deletions, translocations/fusions
detailed_explanation.reason: FISH: fluorescent DNA probes detect amplifications/deletions/translocations on FFPE. HER2/ALK/MYC/1p19q/MDM2/EWSR1 key applications. Break-apart for rearrangements. Single-cell + spatial. Complements IHC + NGS.

scenario: หลักการของ next-generation sequencing (NGS) ใน clinical molecular pathology — workflow + applications + tumor + germline + variant interpretation + limitations
reference_source: AMP/ASCO/CAP 2017 Somatic Variant; ACMG/AMP 2015 Germline; CAP NGS

detailed_explanation.summary:
Clinical NGS Principles:

(1) Concept: massively parallel sequencing — millions of short reads per run vs Sanger one read
detailed_explanation.reason: NGS: massively parallel; library prep → seq → bioinfo → interpretation. Panels (hotspot/CGP/WES/WGS/RNA/ctDNA). Variant tiers — somatic (AMP/ASCO/CAP 2017) + germline (ACMG). Depth + tumor purity essential. RNA needed for fusion detection.

scenario: Lab manager — มี breast biopsy specimen ที่ refrigerated และ formalin fixation เริ่มหลังจาก 3 ชม cold ischemia + ระยะ fixation เพียง 4 ชม ก่อนนำไป processing; ส่ง ER + HER2 — ปัญหา + management
reference_source: ASCO/CAP Breast Biomarker; CAP Cancer Protocol; TJC NPSGs

detailed_explanation.summary:
Pre-analytical Specimen Handling Errors + Mitigation:

(1) CAP/ASCO Guidelines for breast biomarkers (ER, PR, HER2):
  • Cold ischemia time ≤ 1 hour (time from removal to fixation)
detailed_explanation.reason: Breast biomarker preanalytics: cold ischemia ≤1h, fixation 6-72h. Deviation → unreliable ER/HER2. Mitigation: document, comment on report, possibly repeat. QI: workflow + tracking + RCA. Pre-analytic = 60-70% lab errors. Two-identifier rule.

scenario: Intra-operative frozen section — surgeon ขอ frozen section จาก breast lumpectomy margin + sentinel lymph node 3 nodes; lab manager จัด workflow + turnaround time + accuracy expectations

detailed_explanation.summary:
Frozen Section Operations:

(1) Purpose: intraoperative consultation — diagnosis (benign vs malignant), margin assessment, identification of unknown tissue, evaluation of lymph nodes
detailed_explanation.reason: Frozen section: cryostat-cut H&E for intraop diagnosis/margin/LN. TAT target 20 min/block; concordance ≥97%. Limitations: artifacts, thyroid follicular, no IHC. Always 'pending permanent'. QA + clear communication + documentation essential.

scenario: Lab director — มี critical value (panic value) ใน CBC: WBC 0.4 ที่ AM rounds รออยู่ + plt 8; ระบบรายงาน critical values + accountability + กระบวนการ closed-loop communication

detailed_explanation.summary:
Critical Value Reporting (CAP/CLSI/TJC):

(1) Definition: laboratory result that requires immediate notification because it may represent life-threatening condition
detailed_explanation.reason: Critical value: established list + closed-loop reporting. Confirm → notify licensed actionable provider → read-back → time-stamp documentation → escalation if unreachable. < 30 min target. TJC NPSG 02.03.01. Monitor + RCA for misses.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 55 ปี — newly diagnosed lung adenocarcinoma — case discussion ที่ multidisciplinary tumor board: pathology + biomarkers + imaging + treatment planning + clinical trials + multidisciplinary integrative care
reference_source: ASCO; NCI Tumor Board Standards; Temel NEJM

detailed_explanation.summary:
Multidisciplinary Tumor Board (MTB) Integrative Care:

(1) Composition: pathology + radiology + medical oncology + surgical oncology (thoracic) + radiation oncology + pulmonology + palliative + nursing + social work + research/clinical trials + pharmacy + nutrition + supportive care + patient advocacy/navigator
detailed_explanation.reason: MTB integrative care: multidisciplinary team-based decisions. Composition wide. Modern: molecular tumor board, virtual MTB, navigator. QI tracking. Trial integration. Equity + supportive + palliative integration. Patient-centered.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Molecular Tumor Board (MTB) Integrative:

(1) Purpose: interpret complex genomic results, match to targeted therapies/trials, classify variants, recommend additional testing
detailed_explanation.reason: Molecular tumor board: dedicated team for genomic interpretation + trial matching. Variant tiers I-IV (AMP/ASCO/CAP). Germline implications + counseling. Trial matching (MATCH, TAPUR). Access + equity considerations. Integrated with main MTB.

scenario: Hospital autopsy program — sudden unexpected adult death in hospital + family consent given; pathology + multidisciplinary autopsy integrative role in quality + education + family closure

detailed_explanation.summary:
Hospital Autopsy Integrative Value:

(1) Purposes:
  • Diagnostic accuracy: identify missed/incorrect diagnoses (~ 10-30% major discrepancies historically — even in modern era ~ 8-25%)
detailed_explanation.reason: Hospital autopsy: diagnostic accuracy + quality + education + family closure + research + public health. Goldman class discrepancies. Consent + ME jurisdiction. Provisional ~24h, final ~6-8w. Communication with family + clinical team. COVID renewed importance.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 65 ปี — lymph node biopsy: diffuse sheet-like proliferation of large lymphoid cells with vesicular nuclei + prominent nucleoli, high mitoses; IHC: CD20+, CD79a+, BCL6+, MUM1+, CD10-, Ki-67 ~80%, MYC 30%, BCL2 50%; FISH: no MYC/BCL2/BCL6 rearrangements; การวินิจฉัยและการรักษา
reference_source: WHO HAEM5 2022; ICC 2022; NCCN B-cell Lymphomas; POLARIX

detailed_explanation.summary:
Diffuse Large B-cell Lymphoma (DLBCL):

(1) Histology: diffuse effacement by sheets of large transformed B-cells (centroblasts, immunoblasts)
detailed_explanation.reason: DLBCL: large B-cell sheets; Hans algorithm GCB vs non-GCB; FISH for MYC/BCL2/BCL6 to rule out HGBL. R-CHOP standard; Pola-R-CHP new (POLARIX). CAR-T 2nd line (ZUMA-7). Bispecifics + ADCs for R/R. Cure > 60%.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 55 ปี — generalized lymphadenopathy + slow growing → excisional biopsy: nodular/follicular pattern; IHC: CD20+, CD10+, BCL6+, BCL2+ (aberrant in follicles), CD5-, CD23-, Ki-67 low (~10%); FISH: t(14;18) IGH/BCL2 positive; grade 1-2
reference_source: WHO HAEM5 2022; NCCN B-cell Lymphomas; AUGMENT

detailed_explanation.summary:
Follicular Lymphoma (FL):

(1) Histology: nodular/follicular pattern with monotonous small cleaved (centrocytes) + variable large (centroblasts) cells
detailed_explanation.reason: FL: follicular pattern, CD10+/BCL6+/BCL2+ (aberrant in follicles); t(14;18) IGH/BCL2 ~85%. Grading by centroblast count. Asymptomatic → W&W; symptomatic → BR/R-CHOP/R². Tazemetostat (EZH2), CAR-T, bispecifics for R/R. Incurable but indolent.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยชายอายุ 68 ปี — lymphadenopathy + splenomegaly + B symptoms; lymph node biopsy: monotonous medium-sized lymphoid cells with irregular nuclei; IHC: CD20+, CD5+, CD23-, **cyclin D1+**, SOX11+, CD10-; FISH: t(11;14) CCND1/IGH; การวินิจฉัย

detailed_explanation.summary:
Mantle Cell Lymphoma (MCL):

(1) Histology: monotonous medium-sized cells with irregular cleaved nuclei
detailed_explanation.reason: MCL: CD5+/CD23-/cyclin D1+/SOX11+; t(11;14) CCND1/IGH pathognomonic. Variants: classic, leukemic non-nodal (indolent), blastoid (aggressive). TP53 mutated worse; novel/CAR-T. BTKi (incl. pirtobrutinib) + venetoclax for R/R. ZUMA-2 CAR-T.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยชายอายุ 12 ปี — abdominal mass + tumor lysis on presentation; biopsy: monomorphic medium-sized cells with basophilic cytoplasm + vacuoles + 'starry sky' (tingible body macrophages); IHC: CD20+, CD10+, BCL6+, BCL2-, Ki-67 ~100%, MYC+; FISH: t(8;14) MYC/IGH
reference_source: WHO HAEM5 2022; NCCN; Roschewski DA-EPOCH-R Burkitt

detailed_explanation.summary:
Burkitt Lymphoma:

(1) Histology: monomorphic medium-sized cells with basophilic cytoplasm + lipid vacuoles
detailed_explanation.reason: Burkitt: 'starry sky', CD20+/CD10+/BCL6+/BCL2-, Ki-67 ~100%, MYC translocation (t(8;14)). Distinct from HGBL double-hit (BCL2+). TLS prophylaxis essential. DA-EPOCH-R or CODOX-M/IVAC; CNS prophylaxis. Curable.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Classical Hodgkin Lymphoma (CHL):

(1) Histology: Reed-Sternberg (RS) cells — large, multinucleated/bilobed with prominent eosinophilic 'owl-eye' nucleoli
detailed_explanation.reason: Classical Hodgkin: RS cells (CD30+/CD15+/CD20-/CD45-), 'owl-eye' nucleoli + inflammatory background. Subtypes NS/MC/LR/LD. NLPHL (NLPBL) distinct (CD20+). PET-adapted ABVD or A+AVD (ECHELON-1). Brentuximab + IO for R/R. Cure > 80%.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Multiple Myeloma (MM):

(1) Diagnostic criteria (IMWG 2014, updated): clonal BM plasma cells ≥ 10% (or biopsy-proven plasmacytoma) + one of CRAB (hypercalcemia, renal insufficiency, anemia, bone lesions) OR myeloma-defining events (clonal BM PC ≥ 60%, FLC ratio ≥ 100, > 1 focal MRI lesion ≥ 5 mm) — SLiM-CRAB
detailed_explanation.reason: MM: SLiM-CRAB criteria; SPEP/SFLC/IFE + BM + FISH. High-risk t(4;14)/del(17p)/t(14;16)/+1q. Quadruplet Dara-VRd + auto-HCT + maintenance new standard. R/R: BCMA CAR-T (ide-cel/cilta-cel), bispecifics (teclistamab, talquetamab). Supportive bisphos.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Iron Deficiency Anemia (IDA) — Pathology + Workup:

(1) Pattern: microcytic (MCV < 80) + hypochromic (MCH ↓) + elevated RDW (heterogeneous)
detailed_explanation.reason: IDA: microcytic hypochromic + high RDW + low ferritin + low Fe + high TIBC + low sat. vs thal (normal RDW, normal Fe), ACD (low TIBC). Mandatory cause workup (GI in adults). Oral Fe alt-day (Stoffel); IV Fe selected. Monitor ferritin to replete stores.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Vitamin B12 Deficiency Anemia — Pathology + Workup:

(1) Pattern: macrocytic (MCV > 100), megaloblastic (oval macrocytes + hypersegmented neutrophils ≥ 5 lobes + 5%)
detailed_explanation.reason: B12 deficiency: macrocytic + megaloblastic (oval macros + hyperseg neutros); MMA + homocysteine elevated (B12 specific MMA). Pernicious anemia main cause adult. Neuro features irreversible if delayed. Treat IM/high-dose oral; don't give folate alone if B12 low.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 25 ปี African descent — peripheral smear: sickle-shaped RBCs + Howell-Jolly bodies + target cells; Hb electrophoresis: HbS 88%, HbF 8%, HbA2 3.5%, HbA 0%; current VOC + acute chest syndrome; การวินิจฉัยและการรักษา comprehensive

detailed_explanation.summary:
Sickle Cell Disease (HbSS) — Pathology + Management:

(1) Pathophysiology: point mutation HBB gene (Glu6Val) → HbS polymerizes in deoxygenated state → sickle RBCs → vaso-occlusion + chronic hemolysis + endothelial dysfunction + chronic inflammation
detailed_explanation.reason: SCD: sickle RBCs + Howell-Jolly + Hb S on electrophoresis. VOC + ACS major acute. Stroke (TCD screen), sepsis (prophylactic PCN, vaccines). Hydroxyurea foundation; chronic txn for select; allo-HCT + gene therapy (Casgevy CRISPR, Lyfgenia) curative. Voxelotor withdrawn.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Thalassemia (Compound) — Pathology + Management:

(1) Background — common in Southeast Asia + Mediterranean + Africa
detailed_explanation.reason: Thalassemia: severe microcytic + target/teardrop + nRBCs; Hb typing diagnostic; normal/high Fe+ferritin (vs IDA). β-thal major Cooley's, β-thal/HbE common Thai. Tx: transfusion + chelation + luspatercept; allo-HCT + gene therapy (Zynteglo, Casgevy) curative. Prenatal screening Thai policy.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยหญิงอายุ 30 ปี — สงสัย AIHA → DAT (direct antiglobulin test) positive IgG + C3; warm autoantibody; CBC: Hb 7, retic 12%, smear: spherocytes + polychromasia; LDH high, haptoglobin low, indirect bili high

detailed_explanation.summary:
Autoimmune Hemolytic Anemia (AIHA):

(1) Hemolysis markers: anemia + reticulocytosis (compensatory) + LDH up + indirect bili up + haptoglobin low + urine hemosiderin (intravascular)
detailed_explanation.reason: AIHA: hemolysis markers + DAT positive. Warm (IgG, spleen, spherocytes) vs cold (IgM, complement, intravascular). Rule out secondary (lymphoproliferative). Warm: pred → rituximab. CAD: sutimlimab (anti-C1s) + rituximab. Thrombosis prophylaxis.

Blood Culture + Identification Workflow:

(1) Gram stain on positive blood culture — preliminary report ASAP (clusters → Staph
detailed_explanation.reason: Blood culture: gram stain immediate, MALDI-TOF + rapid molecular (BCID2 detects mecA MRSA). Phenotypic AST CLSI/EUCAST. MRSA → vancomycin (not dapto for pulm). Critical-value + stewardship + source control. Contamination < 3% target.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Bacterial Meningitis — Lab + Treatment:

(1) CSF profile:
  • Bacterial: cloudy, WBC > 1000 PMN-predominant, protein > 100, glucose < 40 (CSF/serum < 0.4)
detailed_explanation.reason: Bacterial meningitis: cloudy CSF, PMN, low glucose, high protein. GNDC = N. meningitidis. Empiric ceftriaxone + vanco ± amp (Listeria) + dexamethasone. Meningococcal: reportable, chemoprophylaxis contacts. BioFire ME panel rapid. CT before LP if focal/immunosup.

Liver Function Test (LFT) Interpretation:

(1) Hepatocellular pattern: AST + ALT markedly elevated (> 3-5× ULN)
detailed_explanation.reason: LFT: hepatocellular (AST/ALT↑, R>5) vs cholestatic (ALP/GGT↑, R<2) vs mixed. AST/ALT ratio >2 alcohol. Bili direct vs indirect. Synthetic (albumin, INR). Cirrhosis: MELD, Child-Pugh. Etiology workup: viral/metabolic/autoimmune/drug + imaging. Surveillance HCC/varices.

Transfusion Medicine — Compatibility + Reactions:

  • ABO/Rh typing (forward + reverse — confirm match)

scenario: ผู้ป่วย trauma + hemorrhagic shock — activate massive transfusion protocol (MTP); ratio + monitoring + adjuncts + lab role

detailed_explanation.summary:
Massive Transfusion Protocol (MTP):

(1) Definition: > 10 units PRBC/24 h OR > 4 units in 1 h OR replacement of patient's blood volume
detailed_explanation.reason: MTP: 1:1:1 PRBC:FFP:plt (PROPPR); LTOWB emerging. TXA within 3h (CRASH-2/3). Calcium replacement (citrate chelation). Warm + correct acidosis (lethal triad). TEG/ROTEM-guided. Damage control resuscitation; limit crystalloid. Universal O- emergency.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 35 ปี — recurrent nosebleeds + heavy menses + family history of bleeding → coagulation workup: PT normal, aPTT mildly prolonged, platelet count normal, BT prolonged, vWF antigen low, vWF activity (RCo) low, factor VIII low; การวินิจฉัย

detailed_explanation.summary:
von Willebrand Disease (vWD) — Pathology + Diagnosis:

(1) Background: most common inherited bleeding disorder (~ 1%)
detailed_explanation.reason: vWD: most common inherited bleeding. Types 1 (quantitative partial), 2 (qualitative — 2A/B/M/N), 3 (severe quant). vWF:Ag + RCo + FVIII + multimers + ratio. DDAVP for type 1; vWF concentrate for type 3/severe. TXA adjunct. Acquired vWS exists. Multidisciplinary.

(1) ISN/RPS 2003 (updated 2018) classification:
  • Class I: minimal mesangial — normal LM, mesangial deposits IF/EM
detailed_explanation.reason: Lupus nephritis: ISN/RPS Class I-VI; class IV diffuse proliferative most aggressive. Full-house IF + TR inclusions hallmark. Activity + chronicity scores. Induction: MMF or low-dose CYC + steroids ± belimumab or voclosporin (new). HCQ + RAS blockade + SGLT2.

detailed_explanation.summary:
IgA Nephropathy (Berger's):

(1) Most common primary glomerulonephritis worldwide
detailed_explanation.reason: IgA nephropathy: most common 1° GN; synpharyngitic hematuria. Mesangial IgA-dominant IF + mesangial deposits. Oxford MEST-C scoring. Tx: RAS blockade + SGLT2 + sparsentan + Nefecon (gut-targeted budesonide). Anti-complement emerging. Transplant recurs ~30%.

scenario: Resident question — fundamentals of platelet function + primary hemostasis pathway + clinical platelet function tests + clinical applications

detailed_explanation.summary:
Platelet Function + Primary Hemostasis:

(1) Primary hemostasis steps:
  • Adhesion: vWF binds exposed collagen at injury → platelet GPIb/IX/V binds vWF → tethering
detailed_explanation.reason: Primary hemostasis: adhesion (GPIb-vWF) → activation (granules) → aggregation (GPIIb/IIIa-fibrinogen). Tests: count, PFA, LTA, flow, TEG, VerifyNow. Disorders: thrombocytopenia (qty), Bernard-Soulier/Glanzmann/storage (qual), acquired (drugs/uremia). Tx by disorder.

  • Intrinsic (contact): XII → XIIa → XI → XIa → IX → IXa + VIIIa → X activation
detailed_explanation.reason: Coag: classical waterfall (intrinsic/extrinsic/common) → cell-based model (initiation/amplification/propagation on platelet surface). PT (extrinsic) + aPTT (intrinsic) + TT + mixing study + fibrinogen + anti-Xa + TEG. Anti-thrombin/Protein C-S/TFPI natural. Reversal agents.

scenario: Patient with newly diagnosed acute myeloid leukemia — multidisciplinary integrative care from diagnosis through treatment + survivorship

detailed_explanation.summary:
AML Integrative Multidisciplinary Care:

(1) Diagnosis phase: pathology (morphology + cytochemistry + flow + cytogenetics + molecular FLT3, NPM1, CEBPA, IDH1/2, TP53, KMT2A, RUNX1, ASXL1, comprehensive NGS panel) + hematology + risk stratification (ELN 2022 favorable/intermediate/adverse)
detailed_explanation.reason: AML integrative care: pathology + molecular (ELN 2022) → fitness-based treatment (7+3 + midostaurin vs ven-aza vs targeted). Multidisciplinary + supportive (TLS, infection prophy, transfusion). HCT for adverse. MRD-guided. Survivorship + palliative early.

Chronic Lymphocytic Leukemia (CLL) / Small Lymphocytic Lymphoma (SLL):

(1) Diagnostic criteria (iwCLL 2018): monoclonal B-lymphocyte count > 5 × 10⁹/L in peripheral blood
detailed_explanation.reason: CLL/SLL: CD5+/CD19+/CD23+/CD200+/dim Ig; FMC7-/cyclin D1-/LEF1+. IGHV + FISH (del17p/TP53 high-risk) + NGS. Treat when iwCLL active. Targeted frontline: BTKi (acala/zanu) or ven-obi (CLL14). Pirtobrutinib + CAR-T R/R. Richter transformation, infections.

Extranodal Marginal Zone Lymphoma (MALT lymphoma):

(1) Origin: arises from chronic antigenic stimulation in mucosa-associated lymphoid tissue (MALT) — acquired or normal
detailed_explanation.reason: MALT lymphoma: chronic antigen stimulation (H. pylori/Sjögren/Hashimoto/C. psittaci). Lymphoepithelial lesions + monocytoid B-cells; CD20+/CD5-/CD10-/CD23-. t(11;18) most common — predicts H. pylori eradication resistance. Antibiotic-responsive when feasible.

detailed_explanation.reason: B-ALL: ≥20% blasts; CD19/CD22/CD10/TdT+, MPO-. Risk strat by genetics: hyperdiploid + t(12;21) good; hypodiploid + Ph+ + KMT2A + Ph-like + iAMP21 high. MRD-guided. Pediatric chemo + CNS prophylaxis; +TKI for Ph+. R/R: blinatumomab, inotuzumab, CAR-T.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Myelodysplastic Syndromes (MDS) — Classification + Management:

(1) WHO HAEM5 (2022) / ICC: classified by dysplasia lineage count, blast %, genetics, ring sideroblasts:
  • MDS with low blasts (< 5% BM, < 2% PB)
detailed_explanation.reason: MDS WHO HAEM5: by lineage dysplasia + blast % + genetics (SF3B1, del(5q), TP53). IPSS-M with mutations. Tx by risk: ESA/luspatercept/lenalidomide for low-risk; HMA + allo-HCT for high-risk. TP53 multi-hit worst. Supportive + multidisciplinary.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Chronic Myeloid Leukemia (CML) — BCR-ABL1+:

(1) Diagnostic criteria (WHO HAEM5): BCR-ABL1 fusion (t(9;22)(q34;q11.2) Philadelphia chromosome) — pathognomonic
detailed_explanation.reason: CML: BCR-ABL1 t(9;22) pathognomonic; CP/AP/BP. TKI revolution: imatinib → dasatinib/nilotinib/bosutinib → ponatinib (T315I) → asciminib (STAMP). Molecular monitoring (IS); MMR at 12 mo; TFR for sustained DMR. Switch TKI + kinase mutation testing for failure.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Polycythemia Vera (PV) — WHO 2022:

(1) Diagnostic criteria (3 major or first 2 + minor):
  • Major 1: Hb > 16.5 (M) / 16.0 (F) OR Hct > 49% (M) / 48% (F) OR red cell mass > 25% mean predicted
detailed_explanation.reason: PV: WHO 2022 — Hb/Hct↑ + panmyelosis + JAK2 mutation + low EPO. MPN drivers JAK2/CALR/MPL. Tx: phlebotomy + ASA all; cytoreduction (HU or ropeg-IFN) for high-risk PV. Ruxolitinib for HU-intol/resistant (RESPONSE) + MF. Hct < 45 (CYTO-PV).

reference_source: WHO Head + Neck Tumors 5th ed; AJCC 8th; CAP/ASCO p16

detailed_explanation.summary:
HPV-associated Oropharyngeal Squamous Cell Carcinoma (HPV-OPSCC):

(1) Epidemiology: rising incidence, predominantly white middle-aged men, sexual transmission, oral HPV exposure
detailed_explanation.reason: HPV+ OPSCC: non-keratinizing, p16 block+ (CAP), HPV ISH/PCR confirmatory. AJCC 8th separate staging (better prognosis). 5-yr OS > 80%. Treatment: surgery (TORS) + risk-adapted adjuvant or chemoRT. EBV NPC distinct (EBER ISH). HPV vaccination prevention.

reference_source: WHO Soft Tissue + Bone 5th ed; NCCN GIST; INVICTUS

detailed_explanation.summary:
Gastrointestinal Stromal Tumor (GIST):

(1) Origin: interstitial cells of Cajal (pacemaker cells of GI tract)
detailed_explanation.reason: GIST: CD117+/DOG1+/CD34+ (interstitial cells of Cajal). KIT mutation 75-80% (exon 11 best imatinib response); PDGFRA D842V → avapritinib. SDH-deficient + WT subset. Surgery + adjuvant imatinib for high-risk. Sequential TKIs metastatic (imatinib → sunitinib → regorafenib → ripretinib).

Osteosarcoma (Conventional):

(1) Epidemiology: most common primary malignant bone tumor in adolescents + young adults
detailed_explanation.reason: Osteosarcoma: osteoid by malignant cells; metaphysis adolescent. Subtypes: conventional + telangiectatic + small cell + low-grade (MDM2/CDK4 amp) + parosteal. detailed_explanation.summary:
Ewing Sarcoma:

(1) Epidemiology: bone or soft tissue, children + young adults, diaphysis of long bones / pelvis / chest wall (Askin tumor) / paraspinal
detailed_explanation.reason: Ewing: small round blue cell + glycogen; CD99 membranous + NKX2.2 + FLI1; EWSR1-FLI1 ~85%. 'Ewing-like' (CIC-DUX4, BCOR) distinct entities. Multimodal VDC/IE chemo + surgery/RT. Interval compression pediatric. Lung-only mets better prognosis.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Liposarcoma — Well-Differentiated / Dedifferentiated (WHO 2020):

(1) Categories:
  • Well-differentiated liposarcoma (WDLPS) / atypical lipomatous tumor (ALT) — same biology, different name based on location (extremity = ALT, retroperitoneum/mediastinum/spermatic cord = WDLPS — implies worse prognosis due to inability to resect with margins)
detailed_explanation.reason: Liposarcoma WHO 2020: WDLPS/ALT (MDM2/CDK4 amp 12q), DDLPS (high-grade transition), myxoid (FUS-DDIT3; round cell % matters), pleomorphic. Surgery mainstay; myxoid radiosensitive + trabectedin active. MDM2 IHC/FISH key. Sarcoma multidisciplinary centers.

scenario: Pregnant woman Rh-negative with Rh-positive fetus — postpartum maternal serology + antibody screen + cord blood + Kleihauer-Betke + RhoGAM administration + management of subsequent pregnancy

detailed_explanation.summary:
Rh Alloimmunization Prevention:

(1) Pathophysiology: Rh-negative mother exposed to Rh-positive fetal blood (delivery, miscarriage, trauma, procedures, transfusion) → anti-D Ab → next Rh-positive pregnancy → fetal hemolysis (HDFN — hemolytic disease of fetus + newborn)
detailed_explanation.reason: Rh prevention: RhoIG antenatal (28 wk) + postpartum (if baby Rh+) within 72 h + after sensitizing events. Kleihauer-Betke quantifies FMH for dosing. Already sensitized → MCA-PSV + IUT. Kell-negative blood for Rh-D-neg women childbearing. Other alloantibodies relevant.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Hypothyroidism — Diagnosis + Lab Interpretation:

(1) Primary hypothyroidism: TSH ↑ + free T4 ↓ (overt) or normal (subclinical)
detailed_explanation.reason: Hypothyroidism: primary (TSH↑, T4↓) — Hashimoto most common. Central (low/inappropriate TSH). Sick euthyroid distinct. Biotin/macro-TSH/heterophile interfere. Levothyroxine 1.6 μg/kg empty stomach; recheck q6-8 wk; pregnancy ↑30%. Subclinical Tx criteria.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Hyponatremia — Workup + Treatment:

(1) First step — confirm true hyponatremia + identify acute vs chronic: rule out pseudohyponatremia (hyperlipidemia/hyperproteinemia — older methods
detailed_explanation.reason: Hyponatremia: confirm hypotonic + classify by volume → cause. SIADH if euvolemic + urine osm>100 + urine Na>20 + normal endocrine. Acute symptomatic: 3% saline bolus. Chronic: fluid restriction, tolvaptan, urea. Rate ≤10-12 mEq/L/24h (ODS risk). Re-lower if overcorrected.

detailed_explanation.reason: Cryptococcus: encapsulated yeast (India ink, mucicarmine+); CrAg LFA sensitive. Meningitis AIDS classic. CSF: lymphocytic + ↑OP critical to manage. (1) Concept: cells in suspension pass through laser → detect forward scatter (FSC — size), side scatter (SSC — granularity/complexity), + fluorescence at multiple wavelengths from labeled antibodies binding cellular antigens (surface, intracellular)
detailed_explanation.reason: Flow cytometry: laser detection of size/granularity/fluorochrome-labeled antibodies. Multi-color cocktail + gating (CD45 vs SSC + lineage). Applications: heme malignancy classification, MRD (EuroFlow), PNH (CD55/59/FLAER), CD34, light chain restriction. Compensation + QC.

  • BSL-1: agents not known to cause disease in healthy adults (e.g., E

detailed_explanation.reason: Lab QMS: ISO 15189 / CAP / CLIA / TJC standards; CLSI HS01 12 essentials. Phases: pre-analytical (60-70% errors) + analytical + post-analytical. QC + PT mandatory. Improvement: PDCA/Six Sigma/Lean. RCA + FMEA. KPIs (TAT, critical values, rejections).

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 5 ปี — abdominal mass + LDH high + catecholamine metabolites (HVA + VMA) elevated; biopsy: small round blue cells with Homer-Wright rosettes + neuropil-like background; IHC: synaptophysin+, chromogranin+, PHOX2B+; molecular: MYCN amplification
reference_source: WHO Soft Tissue + Bone 5th ed; COG Neuroblastoma

detailed_explanation.summary:
Neuroblastoma — Pediatric:

(1) Origin: neural crest cells (sympathoadrenal) — adrenal medulla, paraspinal sympathetic ganglia
detailed_explanation.reason: Neuroblastoma: neural crest; Homer-Wright + neuropil; synaptophysin/chromogranin/PHOX2B+; HVA/VMA + MIBG. INRGSS staging + COG risk (age, MYCN amp, ploidy, histology, 1p/11q). High-risk: induction + auto-HCT + anti-GD2 (dinutuximab) maintenance. ALK actionable.

reference_source: WHO Urinary + Male Genital 5th ed; COG Wilms

detailed_explanation.summary:
Wilms Tumor (Nephroblastoma):

(1) Most common renal tumor pediatric
detailed_explanation.reason: Wilms tumor: triphasic (blastemal + epithelial + stromal); favorable vs unfavorable (anaplasia). WT1+/PAX8. Hereditary syndromes: WAGR, Denys-Drash, BWS — surveillance. COG vs SIOP approach (surgery first vs neoadjuvant). Cure > 90% favorable.

scenario: Multidisciplinary integrative pathology — patient with breast cancer + pregnancy + complex management; multidisciplinary tumor board including OB-GYN + medical onc + breast + pathology + maternal-fetal medicine

detailed_explanation.summary:
Pregnancy-Associated Breast Cancer (PABC) Integrative Care:

(1) Definition: diagnosed during pregnancy, lactation, or 1 year postpartum
detailed_explanation.reason: PABC integrative care: multidisciplinary inc. MFM. Trimester-based Tx — surgery anytime; chemo 2nd/3rd OK (anthracycline + taxane), CONTRA in 1st; tamoxifen + trastuzumab CONTRA entire pregnancy; RT post-delivery. Delivery full-term preferred. Outcomes comparable.

choices.text: Cancer of Unknown Primary (CUP) Integrative Care

detailed_explanation.summary:
Cancer of Unknown Primary (CUP) Integrative Care:

(1) Definition: histologically confirmed metastatic cancer without identifiable primary site after appropriate workup
detailed_explanation.reason: CUP integrative care: pathology IHC algorithm + molecular (NGS + tissue-of-origin classifier) → identify favorable subset or actionable target. Treat per primary when identified. Tissue-agnostic targeted therapy (NTRK/MSI-H/BRAF/RET). CUPISCO molecular-guided. Palliative early.

Clinical Lab Role in Public Health Surveillance:

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 35 ปี — nephrotic syndrome (proteinuria 6 g/d + edema + hypoalbuminemia + hyperlipidemia) → kidney biopsy: segmental sclerosis affecting < 50% glomeruli with podocyte foot process effacement on EM; IF: nonspecific IgM/C3 entrapment; APOL1 high-risk variants

detailed_explanation.summary:
Focal Segmental Glomerulosclerosis (FSGS):

(1) Histology: segmental (affecting only portion of glomerulus) + focal (involves only some glomeruli) sclerosis
detailed_explanation.reason: FSGS: segmental sclerosis + podocyte injury. Columbia subtypes (tip, perihilar, cellular, collapsing, NOS). Primary (steroid-responsive, diffuse FPE) vs secondary (maladaptive, focal FPE) vs genetic. APOL1 high-risk variants. Sparsentan + SGLT2 added. Genetic testing selected.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 60 ปี — nephrotic syndrome → biopsy: thickened GBM with subepithelial deposits 'spike' on silver stain; IF: granular IgG + C3 along GBM; IHC: PLA2R+ on glomeruli; serum anti-PLA2R antibody positive

detailed_explanation.summary:
Membranous Nephropathy (MN):

(1) Histology: thickened GBM, 'spikes' on Jones methenamine silver (basement membrane reaction around subepithelial deposits — represents new BM forming around immune deposits)
detailed_explanation.reason: MN: subepithelial deposits + spikes on silver; granular IgG/C3 IF. Primary ~80% — PLA2R, THSD7A, NELL1 (cancer-assoc), exostosins (autoimmune). Secondary: HBV/syphilis/malignancy/drugs/lupus class V. Rituximab first-line (MENTOR); anti-PLA2R titer-guided.

scenario: ผู้ป่วย ICU + AKI; urine: muddy brown granular casts; FENa > 2%; biopsy if obtained: tubular epithelial sloughing + flattened cells + apoptosis + regenerative changes; การวินิจฉัยและการรักษา

detailed_explanation.summary:
Acute Tubular Necrosis (ATN) — Pathology + Management:

(1) Causes:
  • Ischemic: sepsis, hypovolemia, hypotension, cardiac arrest, cardiogenic shock
detailed_explanation.reason: ATN: ischemic or nephrotoxic; muddy brown casts + FENa>2% + isosthenuria. Histology: tubular sloughing + regen. KDIGO AKI staging. AIN distinct (eos, ICI emerging cause). Tx: address cause + supportive + RRT for AEIOU; no specific reversal. Prevention key.

choices.text: Intraductal Papillary Mucinous Neoplasm (IPMN) — Pancreatic Cyst Workup

reference_source: Revised Kyoto 2024; Fukuoka; WHO Digestive Tumors 5th ed

detailed_explanation.summary:
Intraductal Papillary Mucinous Neoplasm (IPMN) — Pancreatic Cyst Workup:

(1) Pancreatic cystic lesions (PCLs) — major categories:
  • Pseudocyst (post-pancreatitis, no epithelium)
detailed_explanation.reason: IPMN: cyst fluid CEA + amylase + glucose + cytology + KRAS/GNAS molecular. MD vs BD subtypes + histologic types. Fukuoka/Kyoto: high-risk stigmata → resect; worrisome → EUS+biopsy. Grade matters (LGD → HGD → invasive). Multidisciplinary; hereditary considerations.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 50 ปี — fatigue + pruritus + jaundice; labs: ALP markedly elevated + GGT high + AMA positive (titer 1:160) + IgM elevated; biopsy: chronic non-suppurative cholangitis + bile duct destruction + lymphoplasmacytic + granulomas; การวินิจฉัย

choices.label: B
choices.text: Primary Biliary Cholangitis (PBC, formerly PBC = primary biliary cirrhosis)

detailed_explanation.summary:
Primary Biliary Cholangitis (PBC, formerly PBC = primary biliary cirrhosis):

(1) Pathophysiology: autoimmune destruction of small + medium intrahepatic bile ducts → chronic cholestasis → fibrosis → cirrhosis
detailed_explanation.reason: PBC: autoimmune small bile duct destruction; women, AMA+ (PDC-E2), ↑ALP. Florid duct lesion + granulomas; Ludwig stages. UDCA 1st; OCA, bezafibrate, seladelpar/elafibranor (new) for incomplete responders. Pruritus management (rifampin, naltrexone, IBAT). Distinct from PSC.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 50 ปี post-cholecystectomy — biopsy bile duct mass: glandular structures with abundant desmoplastic stroma + perineural invasion; IHC: CK7+ CK19+ CK20- TTF1- CDX2- HepPar1- glypican3-; molecular: IDH1 + FGFR2 fusion analyzed

detailed_explanation.summary:
Cholangiocarcinoma — Pathology + Molecular:

(1) Anatomic classification:
  • Intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma (iCCA) — small + large duct
detailed_explanation.reason: Cholangiocarcinoma: iCCA vs perihilar vs distal vs GB. CK7+/CK19+; HepPar-. iCCA molecular: IDH1 (ivosidenib), FGFR2 fusions (pemigatinib/futibatinib), BRAF, HER2, NTRK, MSI-H. Risk: PSC, choledochal cysts, liver flukes (Thailand). Frontline durva+gem-cis (TOPAZ-1).

detailed_explanation.reason: MPM: asbestos; epithelioid/sarcomatoid/biphasic. IHC: calretinin/WT1/D2-40/CK5/6+; CEA/BerEP4/TTF1-. BAP1 loss + MTAP loss + p16/CDKN2A homozygous deletion distinguish from reactive. Nivo+ipi frontline (CheckMate 743). BAP1 germline syndrome.

reference_source: Endocrine Society PPGL 2014/updates; WHO Endocrine Tumors 5th ed

detailed_explanation.summary:
Pheochromocytoma + Paraganglioma (PPGL):

(1) Origin: chromaffin cells of adrenal medulla (pheochromocytoma) or extra-adrenal sympathetic/parasympathetic paraganglia (paraganglioma)
detailed_explanation.reason: Pheo/PGL: Zellballen + chromog/synapto/GATA3+; sustentacular S100+. Plasma/urine metanephrines diagnostic. MIBG + DOTATATE imaging. 40% germline — universal genetic testing (SDHx/VHL/RET/NF1/MAX). Alpha-blockade FIRST pre-op. PRRT (177Lu-DOTATATE) metastatic. Belzutifan VHL.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Immune Thrombocytopenic Purpura (ITP) — Adult:

(1) Diagnosis — diagnosis of exclusion: isolated thrombocytopenia + no other cause + appropriate clinical (no hepatosplenomegaly, no LAD, no systemic illness)
detailed_explanation.reason: ITP: isolated low platelets, exclusion diagnosis; rule out HIV/HCV/H. pylori/lupus/Evans. Tx: observe if ≥30K + no bleed; otherwise dex 40 mg×4 d or pred + IVIG. 2nd line: TPO-R agonists (eltrombopag/avatrombopag), rituximab, splenectomy, fostamatinib. Pregnancy + Evans considerations.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Thrombotic Thrombocytopenic Purpura (TTP):

(1) Classic pentad (not all present): fever + neurologic + thrombocytopenia + microangiopathic hemolytic anemia (MAHA) + renal — only ~ 5% have all
detailed_explanation.reason: TTP: thrombocytopenia + MAHA + ADAMTS13 < 10%. PLASMIC score guides empiric Tx. Differential: HUS (STEC/atypical), drug-induced TMA, pregnancy, DIC. iTTP Tx emergency: TPE daily + steroids + caplacizumab + rituximab. Congenital TTP — recomb ADAMTS13. Multidisciplinary.

scenario: ผู้ป่วย sepsis + multiorgan failure + bleeding from multiple sites + low platelets; labs: PT + PTT prolonged, fibrinogen 80, D-dimer markedly elevated, schistocytes mild on smear; การวินิจฉัยและการรักษา

detailed_explanation.summary:
Disseminated Intravascular Coagulation (DIC):

(1) Pathophysiology: systemic activation of coagulation (tissue factor release) → widespread microvascular thrombosis (organ ischemia) + consumption of clotting factors + platelets → bleeding + thrombosis paradoxically
detailed_explanation.reason: DIC: systemic activation coag → consumption + bleeding + thrombosis. PT/PTT↑ + fibrinogen↓ + D-dimer↑↑ + plt↓ + schistocytes. ISTH score. Triggers: sepsis, APL, OB, trauma. Tx: treat cause + supportive (FFP/cryo/platelets for bleeding; LMWH for thrombotic). APL — ATRA immediate.

Retinoblastoma — Pathology + Integrative Care:

detailed_explanation.reason: Retinoblastoma: pediatric intraocular; Flexner-Wintersteiner rosettes; CRX/synapto+. RB1 germline (40% — hereditary, bilateral, 2nd tumor risk) vs sporadic. Diagnosis clinical (avoid biopsy seeding). Treatment: save life>eye>vision; IV/IAC chemo, focal, enucleation when needed. Germline test + family screening.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Salivary Gland Tumors — Pathology + Classification:

(1) Common benign: pleomorphic adenoma (most common, mixed tumor, PLAG1/HMGA2 fusions, recurrence + malignant transformation risk), Warthin (lymphoid stroma + oncocytic epithelium, smokers), basal cell adenoma, oncocytoma
detailed_explanation.reason: Salivary tumors: MEC (MAML2 fusion), AdCC (MYB-NFIB, PNI), acinic (NR4A3), secretory (ETV6-NTRK3 — larotrectinib!), salivary duct (HER2+/AR+ — trastuzumab/AR-targeted), polymorphous (PRKD1). Milan System FNA reporting. Molecular increasingly drives classification + targeted Tx.

detailed_explanation.reason: Palliative care: integrated early improves QoL + survival (Temel). Pathology contributes via diagnosis + biomarkers + re-biopsy guiding decisions. Multidisciplinary team. Symptom mgmt (pain WHO ladder, dyspnea, nausea, delirium). Goals of care + ACP early. Outpatient/inpatient/hospice models. Equity + education.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Placental Pathology — Workup + Integration:

(1) Indications for placental exam (NIH/CAP/Society for Pediatric Pathology guidelines):
  • Maternal: preeclampsia, eclampsia, gestational diabetes, infection, chorioamnionitis suspicion, severe HTN, abruption, abnormal placentation (accreta/percreta), preterm labor, multiple gestation, antepartum hemorrhage
detailed_explanation.reason: Placental path: indications (maternal/fetal/placental). Amsterdam consensus terminology: acute chorioamnionitis (maternal + fetal stages), MVM (preeclampsia/IUGR), FVM (cord/thrombophilia), VUE, infectious. Twin chorionicity. Multidisciplinary correlation. Counseling for recurrence (preeclampsia, ASA).

Sudden Unexpected Infant Death (SUID) Workflow:

  • SUID — umbrella term for any sudden + unexpected death of infant < 1 yr
detailed_explanation.reason: SUID workflow: scene investigation + doll reenactment + full autopsy + tox + micro + metabolic + molecular autopsy (channelopathy/CM). SIDS = exclusion diagnosis. Triple-risk model. Safe sleep AAP (Back to Sleep). Multidisciplinary review + bereavement. Public health surveillance + equity.

Digital Pathology + AI:

  • Glass slides scanned to high-resolution digital images (gigapixel) at 20×, 40× or higher
detailed_explanation.reason: Digital pathology: WSI + scanner + viewer + AI. Applications: primary dx (FDA-approved devices), teleconsult, quantitative IHC, AI-assisted (Paige Prostate). CAP validation required (≥60 cases). AI considerations: bias, validation, explainability, regulatory, ethics. Foundation models emerging. Augmented intelligence not replacement.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 20 ปี — pancytopenia + recurrent infections; BM biopsy hypocellular; flow cytometry: PNH (paroxysmal nocturnal hemoglobinuria) clone via CD55/CD59/FLAER analysis; การวินิจฉัยและการรักษา
reference_source: Brodsky NEJM PNH; ICCS PNH; APPLY-PNH

detailed_explanation.summary:
Paroxysmal Nocturnal Hemoglobinuria (PNH):

(1) Pathophysiology: somatic PIGA gene mutation in hematopoietic stem cell → deficiency of glycosylphosphatidylinositol (GPI) anchor → loss of GPI-anchored proteins (CD55 + CD59 critical) on cell surface → susceptibility to complement-mediated intravascular hemolysis + thrombosis + bone marrow failure
detailed_explanation.reason: PNH: PIGA mutation → GPI anchor loss → CD55/CD59 deficiency → complement-mediated hemolysis + thrombosis. Flow with FLAER + CD55/CD59 diagnostic. Complement inhibitors: eculizumab, ravulizumab (anti-C5), pegcetacoplan (anti-C3), iptacopan (anti-fB oral), danicopan (anti-fD). Meningococcal vaccine req. Multidisciplinary; pregnancy high-risk.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 35 ปี — recurrent miscarriages + DVT; coagulation workup: prolonged aPTT not corrected on mixing study → lupus anticoagulant positive; anti-cardiolipin IgG/IgM high titer; anti-β2 glycoprotein I positive; criteria + management

detailed_explanation.summary:
Antiphospholipid Syndrome (APS):

(1) Definition (Sapporo + Sydney + 2023 ACR/EULAR criteria revised): clinical + lab criteria:
  • Clinical: vascular thrombosis (arterial, venous, microvascular) OR pregnancy morbidity (≥ 3 unexplained consecutive miscarriages < 10 wk, ≥ 1 unexplained fetal death ≥ 10 wk, ≥ 1 preterm < 34 wk due to severe preeclampsia/eclampsia/placental insufficiency)
detailed_explanation.reason: APS: clinical (thrombosis/preg morbidity) + lab (LA, aCL, aβ2GPI on 2 occasions ≥12 wk apart). Triple positivity highest risk. LA 3-step testing (screen → mix → confirm). Warfarin INR 2-3 (not DOACs for triple+ — TRAPS). Pregnancy: ASA + LMWH. CAPS multiorgan severe. HCQ in lupus.

detailed_explanation.summary:
MSI/MMR Testing:

(1) Mismatch repair (MMR) system: corrects DNA replication errors (mismatched bases, small insertion-deletion loops)
detailed_explanation.reason: MSI/MMR: MMR IHC (4 proteins, paired loss patterns) + MSI PCR (Bethesda/pentaplex). MLH1 loss → reflex BRAF/methylation (CRC). Other losses → germline testing. dMMR/MSI-H predicts IO response (pembro tumor-agnostic, NICHE neoadjuvant CRC). Lynch screening universal CRC+EC. TMB-high related.

detailed_explanation.summary:
PD-L1 Testing:

(1) Background: PD-L1 (programmed death-ligand 1) on tumor + immune cells binds PD-1 on T-cells → immune evasion
detailed_explanation.reason: PD-L1 IHC: TPS (tumor membrane %; NSCLC pembro), CPS (tumor+immune/tumor cells; gastric/H&N/cervical/urothelial/TNBC pembro), IC (immune area %; urothelial atezo). Assay-drug pairs (22C3/28-8/SP142/SP263). SP142 stains less. Pre-analytics + heterogeneity + interpretation pitfalls. CAP PD-L1 guidelines.

Pathology in LMIC + Resource-Limited Settings:

Examples: cervical screening, TB Xpert, HIV VL, sickle cell screening. Lancet Commission. Sustainability + local ownership + equity.

(1) Most common pediatric soft tissue sarcoma
detailed_explanation.reason: Rhabdomyosarcoma: small round blue cells + myogenic (desmin/myogenin/MyoD1+). Subtypes — ARMS (PAX3/7-FOXO1 fusion — aggressive), ERMS (fusion-negative — better), pleomorphic, spindle/sclerosing/MYOD1. Risk strat: fusion status critical. Multimodal VAC + surg + RT.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 60 ปี HIV+ — multiple skin lesions purple-red + lymphadenopathy + visceral involvement; biopsy: spindle cell proliferation + slit-like vascular spaces + hemosiderin; IHC: CD31+, CD34+, ERG+, HHV-8 (LANA)+
reference_source: WHO Skin + Hematolymphoid Tumors; NCI KS

detailed_explanation.summary:
Kaposi Sarcoma (KS):

(1) Etiology: Human Herpesvirus 8 (HHV-8) / Kaposi Sarcoma-associated Herpesvirus (KSHV) — gamma-herpesvirus — infection essential
detailed_explanation.reason: Kaposi sarcoma: HHV-8/KSHV-driven; spindle cells + slit-like vessels + hemosiderin. CD31/34/ERG+/D2-40+ vascular + LANA (HHV-8)+ diagnostic. Variants: classic/endemic/AIDS/iatrogenic. ART backbone AIDS-KS; PLD systemic; pomalidomide new. IRIS flare possible. Other HHV-8 diseases (PEL, MCD) considered.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Castleman Disease — Pathology + Spectrum:

(1) Classification:
  • Unicentric Castleman Disease (UCD) — single lymph node region:
  • Hyaline-vascular variant (most common UCD) — abnormal follicles with onion-skin layers of mantle cells + atretic germinal centers traversed by penetrating sclerotic vessels ('lollipop')
detailed_explanation.reason: Castleman: UCD (hyaline-vascular, plasma cell) vs MCD (HHV-8+ or iMCD incl TAFRO). HHV-8+ MCD — rituximab + antiviral + ART; iMCD — siltuximab (anti-IL-6) first-line. UCD — surgical resection curative. Cytokine storm pathogenesis. POEMS distinct. CDCN criteria.

detailed_explanation.reason: IgG4-RD: systemic fibroinflammatory; multi-organ (pancreatic, biliary, salivary, RPF, kidney, etc.). detailed_explanation.summary:
Electron Microscopy in Pathology:

(1) Concept: uses electron beam (much shorter wavelength than light) → high-resolution imaging of ultrastructure (nanometer-level resolution)
detailed_explanation.reason: EM: ultrastructural resolution; specialized prep (glutaraldehyde, osmium, epoxy, ultra-thin). Applications: renal (GBM, deposits, podocyte FPE, inclusions), nerve/muscle, cilia (PCD), tumor (neuroendocrine granules, melanosomes, Birbeck, mesothelioma microvilli), viral, storage. Largely replaced for tumors but gold-standard in renal/cilia/nerve-muscle.

scenario: Resident — fundamentals of qPCR + ddPCR (droplet digital PCR) for molecular diagnostics — principles + applications + advantages of ddPCR over qPCR + clinical use

detailed_explanation.summary:
qPCR + ddPCR for Clinical Diagnostics:

(1) qPCR (quantitative real-time PCR):
  • Amplifies + quantifies DNA in real-time using fluorescent reporters
detailed_explanation.reason: qPCR: real-time amplification with fluorescent reporters; Ct values; multiplex. ddPCR: droplet partition + end-point + Poisson → absolute quant + higher sensitivity. Applications: BCR-ABL MRD, viral loads (HIV/CMV/HBV), ctDNA mutations (EGFR T790M), MRD solid tumor, NIPT. ddPCR for rare events; qPCR broader use.

detailed_explanation.reason: Lab workforce: CLIA 6-element competency assessment (annual). Continuing ed. Skill mix + staffing + succession + cross-training. DEI + mentorship. Leadership development. detailed_explanation.summary:
Medullary Thyroid Carcinoma (MTC):

(1) Origin: parafollicular C-cells (neuroendocrine — produce calcitonin)
detailed_explanation.reason: MTC: C-cell neuroendocrine; calcitonin+/CEA+/chromog+/TTF-1+; amyloid stroma; RET-driven (germline MEN2 ~25%; sporadic somatic ~75%). Universal germline RET testing. MEN2 screen pheo + HPT before thyroidectomy. Total thyroidectomy + central LN. Selpercatinib/pralsetinib (RET-selective) first-line for advanced.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 45 ปี — small bowel mass — biopsy + IHC: neuroendocrine tumor (well-differentiated NET); chromogranin+, synaptophysin+, Ki-67 5%, CDX2+; staining for serotonin + 5-HIAA elevated in urine; การ stage + grade + Tx
reference_source: WHO Digestive Tumors 5th ed; NETTER-1/2; ENETS

detailed_explanation.summary:
Neuroendocrine Tumor (NET) — Pathology + Management:

(1) WHO classification (2019 updated 2022) — divides by differentiation + grade:
  • Well-differentiated NET (G1, G2, G3 — added in 2017): organoid architecture, uniform cells
detailed_explanation.reason: NET: WHO well-diff (G1/G2/G3 by Ki-67 + mitoses) vs NEC (small/large cell) vs MiNEN. Chromog/synapto/INSM1+; CDX2 site-specific. Functional (carcinoid, insulinoma, ZES) vs non. DOTATATE PET. Tx: surgery + somatostatin analogs + everolimus/sunitinib + Lutathera (PRRT NETTER-1/2) for advanced; platinum-etoposide for NEC.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยหญิงอายุ 50 ปี — pituitary mass + amenorrhea + galactorrhea; prolactin 250 (high); MRI: pituitary macroadenoma; resection: monomorphic chromophobe cells + amyloid; IHC: prolactin+ (specific), other pituitary hormones negative; การวินิจฉัยและการรักษา
reference_source: WHO Endocrine Tumors 5th ed; Endocrine Society

detailed_explanation.summary:
Pituitary Tumors — Pathology + Classification (WHO 2022):

(1) Major categories:
  • Pituitary neuroendocrine tumors (PitNETs) — formerly pituitary adenomas — terminology change reflecting neuroendocrine biology
detailed_explanation.reason: PitNET (WHO 2022 — terminology replaces pituitary adenoma): classified by transcription factor lineage (PIT1, TPIT, SF1) + hormone. Aggressive markers: Ki-67/p53/silent corticotroph/sparsely granulated GH. Prolactinoma — cabergoline first-line (all sizes). Other adenomas — TSS primary. Multidisciplinary.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Plasma Cell Neoplasms — Spectrum + Pathology:

(1) MGUS (Monoclonal Gammopathy of Undetermined Significance) — precursor:
  • M-protein < 3 g/dL
detailed_explanation.reason: Plasma cell neoplasm spectrum: MGUS (precursor) → SMM (high-risk Tx considered) → MM. Solitary plasmacytoma (bone/extramedullary) — RT curative subset. AL amyloidosis — clonal LC + tissue deposition; daratumumab + CyBorD frontline (ANDROMEDA). POEMS (VEGF↑). WM (MYD88+). Imaging WBLD-CT/MRI/PET; mass spec for amyloid typing.

scenario: Resident — fundamentals of clonality testing in lymphoma + leukemia — B-cell + T-cell + NK assessment; PCR-based + flow + NGS approaches

detailed_explanation.summary:
Clonality Testing in Hematopathology:

(1) Concept: lymphoid neoplasms typically arise from single transformed lymphocyte → clonal proliferation → monoclonal antigen receptor (Ig for B-cells, TCR for T-cells)
detailed_explanation.reason: Clonality: B-cell — kappa/lambda restriction (flow + ISH) + Ig gene rearrangement PCR (BIOMED-2 IGH/IGK/IGL) + NGS (clonoSEQ MRD); T-cell — TCR-gamma + beta PCR + NGS (no light chain restriction); NK — difficult. Cautions: pseudoclonality, oligoclonality, plasma cell challenges. EuroClonality standards. Integration with morphology + IHC essential.

detailed_explanation.summary:
SPEP + IFE + sFLC + Hemochromatosis Screening:

(1) Serum Protein Electrophoresis (SPEP):
  • Separates proteins by charge + size on gel/capillary
detailed_explanation.reason: SPEP: albumin → α1/2 → β → γ. M-spike = monoclonal. IFE confirms + types heavy/light chains. sFLC κ/λ ratio sensitive for non-secretory MM + AL amyloid + LC MGUS. Urine IFE for Bence-Jones (largely replaced). Hemochromatosis: TSAT > 45% + ferritin → HFE C282Y/H63D genotype. Phlebotomy treatment + family screening.

scenario: Lab manager — implementing pre-analytic phase improvements (specimen collection + transport + accessioning) — common errors + mitigation + accreditation findings + RCA
reference_source: Plebani Clin Chem Acta; CAP All Common; TJC NPSGs

detailed_explanation.summary:
Pre-Analytic Quality Improvements:

(1) Pre-analytic phase importance:
  • 60-70% of laboratory errors occur here (Plebani)
detailed_explanation.reason: Pre-analytics 60-70% lab errors. Errors: mislabel (most catastrophic), wrong tube/volume, hemolysis, cold ischemia, contamination. Mitigation: 2-ID verification, barcoded wristband, bedside labeling, CLSI order of draw, transport monitoring, electronic systems. Metrics + RCA. CAP/ISO/TJC focus.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 60 ปี — vasculitis workup; biopsy small/medium vessel: necrotizing inflammation; IHC: ANCA-positive (PR3 — cANCA pattern); การจำแนกและการรักษา

detailed_explanation.summary:
ANCA-Associated Vasculitis (AAV) — Pathology + Management:

(1) Categories (CHCC 2012 classification):
  • Granulomatosis with polyangiitis (GPA — formerly Wegener) — necrotizing granulomatous + small/medium vessel vasculitis
detailed_explanation.reason: AAV: GPA (PR3-ANCA, granulomas) + MPA (MPO-ANCA, no granulomas) + EGPA (eosinophils + asthma). Pauci-immune crescentic GN (renal IF: no deposits). PR3/MPO ANCA + IF patterns. Induction: rituximab + reduced-dose steroids (PEXIVAS); avacopan (ADVOCATE) reduces steroid. Maintenance rituximab. Mepolizumab for refractory EGPA.

scenario: Resident — fundamentals of peripheral blood smear review + key morphological findings + when to escalate + integration with CBC results
reference_source: ASH Pocket Companion; Bain Blood Cells Atlas

detailed_explanation.summary:
Peripheral Blood Smear Review:

(1) Indications: abnormal CBC, suspected hematologic disorder, infection, malignancy, anemia workup, pediatric routine, hereditary suspicion
detailed_explanation.reason: PB smear: systematic — WBC diff + RBC (size/shape/color/inclusions) + plt. Key morphology: targets/sickle/schistos/spheros/teardrops/acanthos/Howell-Jolly/basoph stipping/Heinz; Auer rods (AML/APL); hyperseg (B12/folate); smudge (CLL); hairy; Sezary; atypical lymphs (viral). Parasites (malaria, babesia). EDTA platelet clump artifact. Critical → escalate.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Merkel Cell Carcinoma (MCC):

(1) Epidemiology: aggressive cutaneous neuroendocrine carcinoma
detailed_explanation.reason: Merkel cell carcinoma: aggressive neuroendocrine; CK20 paranuclear dot + chromog/synapto/INSM1+; TTF-1 negative. MCPyV+ (80%) vs UV-driven (20%). Avelumab (JAVELIN) + pembro (KEYNOTE-017) + retifanlimab — IO first-line metastatic (revolutionary). WLE + SLN + adjuvant RT for localized. Multidisciplinary.

Synovial Sarcoma:

detailed_explanation.reason: Synovial sarcoma: biphasic/monophasic/poorly diff; TLE1+/keratin+/EMA+; SS18-SSX fusion (t(X;18)) diagnostic; SS18-SSX fusion IHC emerging. scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 50 ปี — neurofibromatosis 1 (NF1) — deep extremity mass + rapid growth → biopsy: hypercellular spindle cell tumor with marked atypia + mitoses + necrosis; IHC: S100 focal+, SOX10 focal+, **H3K27me3 LOSS** (~ 70%), p53 abnormal
reference_source: WHO Soft Tissue + Bone 5th ed; NF1 Guidelines

detailed_explanation.summary:
Malignant Peripheral Nerve Sheath Tumor (MPNST):

(1) Background: 50% arise in NF1 patients (germline NF1 mutation)
detailed_explanation.reason: MPNST: 50% NF1-associated. High-grade spindle + necrosis. IHC: S100/SOX10 focal/patchy; H3K27me3 LOSS (~70%) distinguishes from schwannoma; PRC2 inactivation (SUZ12/EED). NF1 surveillance for transformation (PET-FDG). Wide excision + RT + doxo/ifos chemo. Selumetinib for inoperable plexiform NF1 (prevention).

detailed_explanation.summary:
Primary Mediastinal Large B-cell Lymphoma (PMBL):

(1) Epidemiology: young adults (median 30s), women > men
detailed_explanation.reason: PMBL: young adults, mediastinal/thymic origin. Large B-cells in sclerotic compartments. CD20+/CD23+/CD30 variable/MAL+; CD15-. 9p24.1 amp → PD-L1/L2/JAK2 → IO sensitivity. DA-EPOCH-R without RT preferred (Wilson) — > 90% cure. PD-1 inhibitors + CAR-T for R/R. Grey zone lymphoma distinct.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 30 ปี Lupus + nephritis + hemoptysis → kidney + lung biopsies; renal IF: linear IgG along GBM; serum anti-GBM (alpha-3 NC1 collagen IV) positive; การวินิจฉัย

detailed_explanation.summary:
Anti-GBM (Goodpasture) Disease:

(1) Pathogenesis: autoantibodies against α3 chain of type IV collagen (Goodpasture antigen) in glomerular + alveolar basement membranes → linear deposition + complement activation → glomerulonephritis + pulmonary hemorrhage
detailed_explanation.reason: Anti-GBM (Goodpasture): anti-α3 type IV collagen → linear IgG along GBM (pathognomonic). RPGN + pulm hemorrhage. Test anti-GBM + ANCA (double-positive ~20-40%). Tx EMERGENCY: PLEX daily + cyclophos + steroids; high recurrence transplant if active. Monitor titers; dialysis at presentation = poor renal recovery.

scenario: ผู้ป่วย septic shock — ABG: pH 7.20, pCO2 25, HCO3 10, anion gap 25, lactate 6; mixed acid-base disorder + interpretation + Tx

detailed_explanation.summary:
Acid-Base + Lactic Acidosis Interpretation:

(1) Stepwise approach to ABG:
  • Step 1: Acidemia or alkalemia? pH (normal 7.35-7.45)
detailed_explanation.reason: ABG: pH → metab vs resp → compensation (Winters: expected pCO2 = 1.5×HCO3+8) → AG → delta-delta. HAGMA causes: MUDPILES/GOLDMARK incl lactic acidosis (sepsis = Type A). Treat underlying + sepsis bundles. Lactate trend prognostic. Bicarb controversial (severe + persistent). NAGMA: USED CAR P.

scenario: Resident — fundamentals of mass spectrometry in clinical pathology (LC-MS/MS for steroid hormones + vitamin D, MALDI-TOF for microbe ID + protein typing) applications

detailed_explanation.summary:
Mass Spectrometry in Clinical Path:

(1) Principles: mass spec ionizes molecules → separates ions by mass-to-charge (m/z) ratio → detector counts
detailed_explanation.reason: Mass spec: LC-MS/MS (small mol — steroids/TDM/tox/newborn screen), MALDI-TOF (large mol — micro ID + amyloid typing). Higher specificity than immunoassay (gold standard for steroids in women/children + amyloid typing). MS revolutionized clinical micro + amyloid Dx. Equipment + expertise barriers but expanding clinical use.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 50 ปี ABG + electrolytes — Na 142, K 2.8, Cl 92, HCO3 35; BP 180/110; renin low, aldosterone high; primary aldosteronism workup

detailed_explanation.summary:
Primary Aldosteronism (PA, Conn Syndrome):

(1) Most common secondary HTN — under-recognized — ~ 5-15% of HTN
detailed_explanation.reason: Primary aldosteronism: ↑aldo + ↓renin + ↑ARR. Causes: APA (~35%, surgical), BIH (~60%, medical), familial. Screening — ARR + drug interference considered. Confirm with saline infusion. AVS gold standard for lateralization. Tx: laparoscopic adrenalectomy (APA) or MRA (spironolactone/eplerenone). Aldo synthase inhibitors emerging.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 40 ปี — Cushingoid features + weight gain + central obesity + striae + HTN + hyperglycemia; 24-h urine free cortisol elevated + late-night salivary cortisol elevated; ACTH suppressed; workup

detailed_explanation.summary:
Cushing Syndrome Workup:

(1) Definitions:
  • Cushing syndrome (CS) — clinical state of chronic glucocorticoid excess (any cause)
detailed_explanation.reason: Cushing syndrome: ↑cortisol — iatrogenic vs endogenous (ACTH-dep: pituitary or ectopic; ACTH-indep: adrenal). Screen: late-night salivary, 24h UFC, 1-mg DST. Confirm with ≥2 tests + exclude pseudo-Cushing. ACTH guides further: high-dose DST + IPSS for source. Tx: TSS for Cushing disease; adrenalectomy for adenoma; treat ectopic primary; osilodrostat medical management.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Viscoelastic Hemostasis Testing (TEG + ROTEM):

(1) Concept: assesses global hemostasis in whole blood — clot formation + dynamics + strength + fibrinolysis — in real-time, near patient
detailed_explanation.reason: TEG/ROTEM: viscoelastic whole-blood hemostasis. Parameters — R/CT (factors), K/CFT + α (fibrinogen/platelets), MA/MCF (clot strength), LY30/LI (lysis). FIBTEM isolates fibrinogen. Applications: trauma, cardiac surgery, liver tx, OB hemorrhage, ICU, DIC. Faster + functional + reduces blood product overuse. Guideline-recommended for major bleeding.

scenario: Lab — implementing patient blood management (PBM) program — preoperative anemia + intraoperative blood conservation + restrictive transfusion + multidisciplinary integration

detailed_explanation.summary:
Patient Blood Management (PBM):

(1) WHO definition: 'a patient-centered, evidence-based + systematic approach to optimize the management of patients + transfusion of blood products for quality + effective patient care' — three pillars:

(2) Three pillars of PBM:
  • Pillar 1: Optimize erythropoiesis (treat anemia, support red cell mass):
  • Pre-operative anemia diagnosis + treatment: address...
detailed_explanation.reason: PBM 3 pillars: optimize erythropoiesis (preop anemia Tx — iron/EPO), minimize blood loss (TXA, cell salvage, ANH, surgical technique, viscoelastic-guided), harness physiologic tolerance (restrictive Hb 7-8 + single-unit). WHO endorsed. Reduces transfusion 20-50% + costs + adverse events. Multidisciplinary + protocols + audit. Cultural sensitivity.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 70 ปี — Paget disease bone — biopsy: disorganized lamellae + mosaic pattern + numerous osteoclasts + woven + lamellar bone; serum ALP markedly elevated + normal Ca; การวินิจฉัยและการรักษา + risk of malignant transformation

detailed_explanation.summary:
Paget Disease of Bone:

(1) Pathophysiology: focal disorder of accelerated bone turnover — increased osteoclast activity → resorption → reactive osteoblast activity → chaotic disorganized 'mosaic' bone deposition
detailed_explanation.reason: Paget disease bone: increased turnover with mosaic cement lines + osteoclast prominence. ALP markedly ↑ with normal Ca/P. Imaging: cortical thickening, bone expansion, lytic-sclerotic. SQSTM1 mutations ~30-50%. Tx zoledronic acid IV once if indicated (pain, surgery, complications). Malignant transformation 1-5% (osteosarcoma, GCT) — sudden pain warning.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 25 ปี — anaplastic large cell lymphoma → biopsy lymph node: large pleomorphic cells + 'hallmark cells' with horseshoe nuclei; IHC: CD30+, ALK+, CD3+, CD2+, CD43+, EMA+; การวินิจฉัย และการรักษา

choices.label: B
choices.text: Anaplastic Large Cell Lymphoma (ALCL) Subtypes (WHO HAEM5)

detailed_explanation.summary:
Anaplastic Large Cell Lymphoma (ALCL) Subtypes (WHO HAEM5):

(1) Categories:
  • Systemic ALK-positive ALCL — younger (< 30), better prognosis
detailed_explanation.reason: ALCL subtypes: systemic ALK+ (better), ALK- (DUSP22 better, TP63 worse), pcALCL (indolent), BIA-ALCL (implant — surgery curative early). CD30+ + hallmark cells + EMA+. NPM-ALK most common ALK+ fusion. A+CHP (brentuximab + CHP) frontline (ECHELON-2). ALK inhibitors for ALK+ relapsed. Variable prognosis by subtype.

scenario: Resident — fundamentals of point-of-care testing devices + glucose meters + ABG analyzers + coagulation POC (INR + aPTT) + clinical performance vs central lab + QC
reference_source: CLSI POCT; CAP POCT Checklist; CLIA

detailed_explanation.summary:
Point-of-Care Testing (POCT) Devices:

(1) Glucose meters:
  • Capillary blood (finger stick) testing — most common POCT
detailed_explanation.reason: POCT: glucose meters (within 10-15% central lab; finger stick issues), ABG (i-STAT — rapid critical care), INR meters (anticoag clinics), ACT (heparin monitoring), rapid Ag (strep/flu/COVID), troponin/BNP, bilirubin. QC + competency + linkage to LIS. CLIA waived vs mod-complexity. Wearable + AI + multiplex future.

scenario: Integrative — pathology + ethics + research — informed consent + tissue + data use + research participation + Tuskegee + Henrietta Lacks + modern frameworks
reference_source: Belmont Report; Common Rule 2018; ACMG SF

detailed_explanation.summary:
Research Ethics in Pathology:

(1) Historical context — landmark events shaping modern ethics:
  • Tuskegee syphilis study (1932-1972) — Black men with syphilis observed without informed consent + denied treatment
detailed_explanation.reason: Research ethics: Tuskegee + Henrietta Lacks shaped modern framework. Belmont Report — respect/beneficence/justice. Informed consent (capacity + voluntary + information + understanding + documented). Tissue/data use specific consent. Common Rule 2018 + HIPAA. Equitable participation + special populations. Genomics secondary findings + GINA. AI + ethics emerging. Multidisciplinary IRB + community.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 50 ปี — adrenal incidentaloma 5 cm on CT — workup + differential + pathology + multidisciplinary integrative management
reference_source: Endocrine Society Adrenal 2016; ESE Guidelines; AACE

detailed_explanation.summary:
Adrenal Incidentaloma — Workup + Management:

(1) Definition: adrenal mass discovered incidentally on imaging done for other reasons
detailed_explanation.reason: Adrenal incidentaloma: question 1 — functional? (1-mg DST, metanephrines, ARR + selected) + question 2 — malignant? (size > 4cm + suspicious imaging). NEVER biopsy pheochromocytoma + ACC. Surgery for functional, large, suspicious. ACC: surgical resection + mitotane + EDP chemo. Weiss criteria + IHC (SF1/MelanA/inhibin+). Genetic associations.

scenario: Integrative pathology + One Health — emerging infectious disease, antimicrobial resistance, zoonosis surveillance — multidisciplinary cross-sector framework + Thailand context

detailed_explanation.summary:
One Health Framework — Pathology Contribution:

(1) One Health definition: collaborative + multisectoral + transdisciplinary approach recognizing the interconnection between human + animal + environmental health to achieve optimal health outcomes
detailed_explanation.reason: One Health: integrated human + animal + environmental health; > 60% emerging infections zoonotic. Pathology roles: diagnostic capacity, surveillance, reference, research, capacity-building. AMR: WHO GLASS, stewardship multi-sector, R&D. Thailand specific: NE Thai cholangio + Opisthorchis, leptospirosis, rabies, artemisinin resistance Mekong. COVID lessons reinforced need.
